data.body:
PASS 1 review (G1G2 — edit-set / scope accuracy), Cluster 1 plan (current revision). Re-grounded every named target via Read/Grep against this plan text. Note: the current plan ALREADY specifies `ascii_only=True, default=str` for conflict_resolver._hash_value with the full ensure_ascii reasoning — the prior-cycle byte-divergence finding is RESOLVED in this revision; not re-raised.

AFFIRMATIONS (G1G2 criteria this chunk passes):
- No hallucinated/missing edit targets. All five named symbols exist exactly: `_store/canonical.py::canonical_str`/`canonical_bytes` (canonical.py:39-47, with the claimed `ensure_ascii=False, sort_keys=True, separators=(",",":")` defaults and positional `event` signature); `signing.py::_canonical_payload` (signing.py:187-199, `ensure_ascii=False`); `llm/workflow/schema.py::canonical_json`/`content_hash` (schema.py:390-403, `ensure_ascii=False`); `conflict_resolver.py::_hash_value` (conflict_resolver.py:215-219, OMITS `ensure_ascii` → stdlib default True, uses `default=str`); `mutation.py::serialize_manifest` (mutation.py:89-108, `ensure_ascii=True`). Every per-site byte claim in the plan is factually correct.
- The six "untouched/positional" importers are correctly enumerated AND all genuinely call positionally (so keyword-only additions don't reach them): event_append.py:114, txn.py:220/394/415, delete.py:114/205, compact.py:139, inbound_translate.py:239, batch_dispatch.py:238.
- Cross-package import direction is sound (a plausible scope hazard that resolves in the plan's favor): conflict_resolver.py/mutation.py live under `_engine/rebar_reconciler/` and import siblings via the bare `rebar_reconciler.*` namespace, but `from rebar._store.canonical import ...` already has live precedent in the SAME package (inbound_translate.py:218, batch_dispatch.py:211) — no new boundary leak.
- `schema.canonical_json`/`content_hash` re-export surface enumerated and addressed: both are in `llm/workflow/__init__.py.__all__` (:56-57, :85-86).
- Behavioral classification: all four hunks are IN-SCOPE refactors (relocate existing encoding onto the seam; no new behavior). No CREATION/out-of-scope hunk; AC forbids a new module and freezes output bytes.
- Adjacent NOT-in-scope sites correctly excluded (no false-positive scope creep): `llm/prompts.py::prompt_content_hash` (:642-645) is `sha256(text.encode())` over a STRING, not sorted-key JSON — not a member of the "sorted-key compact JSON" family, rightly omitted.

FINDINGS:

- [ ] Enumerate the OUTSIDE-dir consumers of the two non-`_hash_value` migrated symbols whose existing tests pin the exact output the seam must preserve, so "make test passes" is grounded in named pins rather than only the new snapshot test — and note the shape mismatch for `serialize_manifest`. For `mutation.serialize_manifest`: tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/mutate/test_mutation.py:263-322 (100-permutation stability, sort-key order, `hash == sha256(json_text)`) and tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_differ.py:299-438. Critically, `serialize_manifest` returns `tuple[str,str]` (json_text, hash) and keeps its own item-shaping/sort locally — the seam replaces only the terminal `json.dumps(..., ensure_ascii=True)`+`sha256`, so AC #6's byte-equality assertion must pin the tuple's `json_text` (via `canonical_str(items, ascii_only=True)`), not a `content_hash(doc)`-only check. For `schema.canonical_json`/`content_hash`: tests/unit/workflow/test_schema.py:214-218 (order-independence, len==64) — these call `wf.content_hash`/`canonical_json` from OUTSIDE schema.py and must still resolve via the preserved re-export.
  - criteria: G1G2
  - location: plan AC line 6 (characterization test) and the "Migrate each site" bullets for mutation.py / schema.py; tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/mutate/test_mutation.py:263-322; tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/diffing/test_differ.py:299-438; tests/unit/workflow/test_schema.py:214-218
  - evidence: `serialize_manifest` (mutation.py:89-108) returns `tuple[str,str]` and shapes/sorts `items` itself; the seam can only replace its inner `json.dumps`+`sha256`, and the existing pins assert on `json_text` — a `content_hash`-only parity check would not cover the manifest TEXT bytes those tests lock. These test consumers live outside both artifact dirs and are not enumerated in the plan.

- [ ] Acknowledge (or explicitly de-scope with one line) the OTHER sorted-key JSON sites grep finds, so the "single home for sorted-key compact JSON" scope boundary is unambiguous and a future audit can see they were considered, not missed: `llm/aggregate.py:114` (`json.dumps(cit, sort_keys=True)` — no compact separators), `llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py:741` (`json.dumps(basis, sort_keys=True, ensure_ascii=False)` — no compact separators), `llm/plan_review/attest.py:44,47` (`json.dumps(..., sort_keys=True)`), `llm/workflow/bpmn.py:129,221` (`json.dumps(..., sort_keys=True)`). These differ in shape (no `separators=(",",":")` and/or no sha256) so excluding them from the "sorted-key COMPACT JSON (+sha256)" family is defensible — but the plan's "four real duplicate sites" claim states the count without naming why these near-neighbors are out, leaving the scope boundary implicit.
  - criteria: G1G2
  - location: plan "What" header ("the four real duplicate sites") and "Why" ("Five near-identical canonical encoders"); src/rebar/llm/aggregate.py:114; src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py:741; src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py:44,47; src/rebar/llm/workflow/bpmn.py:129,221
  - evidence: grep for `json.dumps(...sort_keys=True...)` across src/rebar returns these additional sites beyond the four named; each lacks the compact `separators` and/or the sha256 wrapper that define the targeted family, so they are plausibly out-of-scope — but that exclusion rationale is not stated, so a reader cannot distinguish "deliberately scoped out" from "unenumerated consumer."
